phases.name: Wire window-cropped capture and degrade audio gracefully
phases.description: Fix the greyed Share button: (B) wire the window-cropped mode so the record page actually runs startWindowCroppedRecording, and (A) retry without audio when getDisplayMedia rejects so a surface that cannot supply audio no longer leaves Share disabled.

steps.name: No-audio retry fallback for getDisplayMedia (RC-A) + regression test
steps.description: In src/record.ts, when getDisplayMedia with audio rejects (Share disabled / NotAllowedError-style on a surface that cannot supply audio), retry the SAME displaySurface with audio:false before surfacing the cancel notice. Apply to (1) startWindowCroppedRecording's WINDOW_CROPPED_CONSTRAINTS path and (2) the live tab-path fallback chain at record.ts ~326-333. Upholds Decision B: audio is kept when available, dropped only when unsupplyable. Firefox/single-tab byte-for-byte unchanged. The injectable WindowCroppedRecordingDeps.getDisplayMedia is the regression-test seam.
steps.criteria: Given a fake getDisplayMedia that REJECTS the audio:true call then RESOLVES a no-audio call, when startWindowCroppedRecording runs, then it returns a started session, getDisplayMedia is called twice, the second call carries audio:false (same displaySurface:'window'), and onStateChange('recording') fires. Given a fake getDisplayMedia that rejects BOTH calls, then it returns null, a visible notice is set, onStateChange('idle') fires, and no session/download is created. The existing audio-success path (single call, audio:true) is unchanged. npm run build green; full vitest suite green with zero regressions.
steps.scenario_name: getDisplayMedia degrades to audio:false when the audio request is unsatisfiable, so Share is no longer greyed; both-reject still cancels cleanly (RC-A regression)

steps.name: Wire ?mode=window-cropped so the record page runs the window-cropped path (RC-B) + routing test
steps.description: Fix the dead-code root cause: the popup opens a bare record.html, so bootstrapRecordPage always runs the tab path (startRecording) and never startWindowCroppedRecording. (1) src/popup.ts: append ?mode=window-cropped to the record.html URL when the selected mode is window-cropped. (2) src/record.ts bootstrapRecordPage: read the mode from location, and when window-cropped, wire the action button to startWindowCroppedRecording with composeFromGranted built from the live preview <video> + compositor <canvas> + crop selection (composeFromPreview), instead of startRecording. Single-tab/Firefox routing (no mode param) unchanged. Provide a pure/headless routing seam so the decision is testable without the real picker.
steps.criteria: Given the popup with window-cropped selected, when it opens the record page, then the record.html URL carries mode=window-cropped. Given bootstrapRecordPage on a page whose mode is window-cropped with an injected getDisplayMedia, when the action button is clicked, then startWindowCroppedRecording is invoked (getDisplayMedia called with displaySurface:'window') and NOT the tab-path startRecording. Given no mode param (single-tab/Firefox), then the existing startRecording path runs unchanged. The full crop-and-record pixel flow remains @human-gate dogfood. npm run build green; full vitest suite green with zero regressions.
steps.scenario_name: popup passes mode=window-cropped and the record page routes the action button to startWindowCroppedRecording (not the tab path) under that mode (RC-B regression)

validation.review_note: 2-step bugfix per /nw-bugfix Phase 3; RC-A (audio degrade) then RC-B (mode wiring). User approved both parts.
